channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: 10,000 pages of records about Robert F. Kennedy's 1968 assassination are released, on Trump's order
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — About 10,000 pages of records related to the 1968 assassination of Sen. Robert F. Kennedy were released Friday, including handwritten notes by the gunman, who said the Democratic presidential candidate “must be disposed of” and acknowledged an obsession with killing him.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK and HAYA PANJWANI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Deliberations begin at bribery trial of Nadine Menendez, wife of former US Sen. Bob Menendez
item.description: A jury has begun deliberating in the bribery case brought against Nadine Menendez after a prosecutor told a jury that she was a “critical participant” in a five-year fraud with her husband, former New Jersey Sen. Bob Menendez.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ryan Gosling to star in next 'Star Wars' film, Lucasfilm announces
item.description: The Force was with many Japanese, as well as visitors from abroad, at a “Star Wars” event on Friday where Lucasfilm announced the next installation in the film franchise.
item.dc:creator: Yuri Kageyama, ABC 4

item.title: Trump calls FSU shooting a ‘shame,’ says he’ll always protect Second Amendment
item.description: President Trump on Thursday called a deadly shooting at Florida State University (FSU) "terrible," but he dismissed the possibility of it spurring any changes to gun laws in the country. "Look, I'm a big advocate of the Second Amendment. I have been from the beginning.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's claims about Abrego Garcia’s gang ties largely rely on 1 confidential tip
item.description: The Trump administration’s argument that the mistakenly deported Kilmar Abrego Garcia is a member of MS-13 is based largely on a confidential tip he and his family have long disputed.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Oklahoma City to mark 30 years since the bombing that killed 168 people and shook America
item.description: A bomb with a force powerful enough to instantly destroy much of a nine-story building shattered a quiet Oklahoma City morning and sent a shock wave through America.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump admin to cut 90% of CFPB in latest layoffs: Reports
item.description: President Donald Trump’s administration is cutting nearly 90 percent of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau’s (CFPB) employees in a push to significantly downsize the watchdog agency formed to shield U.S. consumers from financial fraud and abuse.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: More details emerge about Florida State University shooting suspect Phoenix Ikner as motive remains a mystery
item.description: More details are emerging Friday about Florida State University shooting suspect Phoenix Ikner as law enforcement is trying to determine the motive of the attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Marines killed in military vehicle crash identified
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (KTSM) - Officials with the 1st Marine Division identified the two Marines who were killed after a military vehicle crash on Tuesday, April 15, while supporting Joint Task Force Southern Border operations.
item.dc:creator: Luisa Barrios, NewsNation

item.title: Maryland Sen. Van Hollen meets with Abrego Garcia in El Salvador amid court fight over US return
item.description: SAN SALVADOR (AP) — Maryland Sen. Chris Van Hollen met with Kilmar Abrego Garcia on Thursday evening in El Salvador, coming face to face with the wrongly deported man after two days in the country pushing for his release.
item.dc:creator: YOLANDA MAGAÑA and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Video shows 'Call on Jesus' bus explode in ball of flames along Louisiana road
item.description: Authorities are investigating after a commuter bus wrapped with the message "Call on Jesus" caught fire Wednesday, sparking a massive explosion in North Shreveport, Louisiana.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Cheng, Fox 5

item.title: Doctor in Germany accused of 15 murders, suspected in 95 more
item.description: A 40-year-old doctor practicing palliative medicine in Germany has been charged with killing 15 of his patients and is suspected in another 95 cases, NBC News reported.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KTLA 5

item.title: The NFL Draft is one week away; 49ers, Raiders eye top-valued picks
item.description: The 2025 NFL Draft begins on April 24 and the San Francisco 49ers and the Las Vegas Raiders both have a good shot at snagging top-10 valued talent.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, KRON4

item.title: Military officials identify two Marines killed in crash in New Mexico
item.description: Two Camp Pendleton Marines killed in a crash during a convoy movement in New Mexico Tuesday have been identified.
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, KTLA 5

item.title: Ohio man arrested after investigators discover IED, videos, photos of bomb-making materials
item.description: An Ohio man was arrested and charged with possessing a destructive device after an investigation into an IED uncovered a trove of video and photo evidence.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration takes aim at Harvard's international students and tax-exempt status
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has escalated its ongoing battle with Harvard, threatening to block the university from enrolling international students as the president called for withdrawing Harvard’s tax-exempt status.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, FATIMA HUSSEIN and ALIA WONG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FSU lecturer finds broken doors in building after 2-hour shooting lockdown
item.description: Busted doors and debris were what a lecturer at Florida State University found after being locked down for hours during Thursday's mass shooting.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, KRON4

item.title: Lawmakers move to mandate the display of Ten Commandments and ban pride flags in Alabama schools
item.description: MONTGOMERY, Ala. (AP) — Alabama lawmakers approved a slew of bills on Thursday that would expand the use of Christian texts in public schools and limit protections for LGBTQ+ students, in lockstep with a national conservative agenda that has divided statehouses across the country.
item.dc:creator: SAFIYAH RIDDLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: UnitedHealthcare killing suspect Luigi Mangione indicted as prosecutors push for death penalty
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Luigi Mangione was indicted Thursday on a federal murder charge in the killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, a required step as prosecutors work to make good on the Trump administration’s order to seek the death penalty for what it called a “premeditated, cold-blooded assas
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Facebook sending out $40 payments to users over 'Like button' issue
item.description: The lawsuit claimed Facebook was using cookies to collect data on users in 2010 and 2011, even when they were logged out or visiting other websites.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, NewsNation

item.title: New Pizookie coming to BJ’s Restaurants
item.description: The Broccoli Cheddar Pizookie, slated to debut at BJ’s Restaurant & Brewhouse nationwide, won’t be coming to restaurants after all. Instead, BJ’s will be releasing a Snickers Pizookie. “We've come to our senses.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Deer Valley Music Festival to feature Lindsey Stirling, Beach Boys, and Common
item.description: The 2025 Deer Valley Music Festival lineup has now been announced, featuring big names on the lineup — such as The Beach Boys, Lindsey Stirling, and Common.
item.dc:creator: Kayla Baggerly, ABC 4

item.title: FSU shooting: Location offers insight into gunman's motive
item.description: Authorities identified the suspect as 20-year-old student Phoenix Ikner, the son of a Leon County sheriff's deputy.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Jury selection heads into next week at Weinstein rape retrial
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Dozens more people were screened Thursday as potential jurors for Harvey Weinstein's New York rape retrial, but the selection wrapped up for the week with several slots still to be filled.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Train collides with funeral home limo in Tennessee; family of 6 injured
item.description: A funeral limo en route to a funeral — and carrying a family to the funeral — was struck by a train on Thursday in Tennessee.
item.dc:creator: David Royer, KRON4

item.title: Trump calls FSU shooting a ‘shame,’ says he’ll always protect Second Amendment
item.description: President Trump on Thursday called a deadly shooting at Florida State University (FSU) “terrible,” but he dismissed the possibility of it spurring any changes to gun laws in the country.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: White House proposes eliminating Head Start funding as part of sweeping budget cuts
item.description: The Trump administration is asking Congress to eliminate funding for Head Start, a move that would cut early education for more than half a million of the nation’s neediest children and child care for their families.
item.dc:creator: JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Luigi Mangione assassination case signals DOJ shift on death penalty in blue states, expert says
item.description: Luigi Mangione's defense is ramping up its fight against the death penalty early in a federal case in the alleged assassination of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Arson attack probe at Pennsylvania governor's mansion looking into suspect's hatred of Josh Shapiro
item.description: HERSHEY, Pa. (AP) — Authorities investigating why a man set fire to Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro’s official residence are probing whether the suspect was motivated by the Democrat's Jewish faith or positions on Israel’s war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Officials in Puerto Rico demand answers after another blackout leaves 1.4 million without power
item.description: Crews worked early Thursday to restore power to Puerto Rico after a blackout across the entire island that affected the main international airport, several hospitals and hotels filled with Easter vacationers.
item.dc:creator: Dánica Coto, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tennessee’s GOP leads the fight to deny public education to children without documents
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — Damian Felipe Jimenez has many dreams about his future — he could be a restaurant owner, a scientist or maybe something else.
item.dc:creator: KIMBERLEE KRUESI and KRISTIN M. HALL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NY prison guards beat an inmate to death then tried to cover it up, prosecutors say
item.description: UTICA, N.Y. (AP) — Ten New York prison guards were charged Wednesday in connection with the fatal beating of a 22-year-old inmate last month — including two charged with murder. It's the second time a group of correctional officers in the state was indicted for a death behind bars this year.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL and DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Lost toddler found miles from home by dog that guided him to safety, sheriff says
item.description: When an Arizona toddler wandered away from home Monday, a team of deputies, search and rescue volunteers and rangers began their search. In the end, it was a protective pup who track down the 2-year-old.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WGN 9

item.title: St. Pete woman reunites with nurse who saved her life after crash
item.description: A Pinellas County woman got the chance to reunite with the nurse who helped save her life after a crash two weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Marino, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Family says ICE agents smashed car window in seizing Guatemalan man who's seeking asylum
item.description: NEW BEDFORD, Mass. (AP) — A Massachusetts family is demanding answers from U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement, complaining its agents smashed a car window with a hammer and detained a man who they claim had applied for asylum.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY and RODRIQUE NGOWI, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Zoom service restored after outage affects thousands
item.description: Thousands of Zoom users reported issues accessing virtual meetings Wednesday, with the site confirming shortly after noon it was investigating the issue.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KTLA 5

item.title: ACLU sues Defense Department school system over banning race- and gender-related books
item.description: The American Civil Liberties Union has sued the Department of Defense’s school system for children of military families, asserting that the removal of race- and gender-related books and curricula violated students’ First Amendment protections against government censorship.
item.dc:creator: OLIVIA DIAZ, Associated Press, ABC 4
